message:
refactor(rpc): simplify extension command resolution, add busy-command rejection test

Simplify the extension command resolution path in the prompt handler by
passing `rpc_extension_manager` directly to `resolve_extension_command`
instead of acquiring a session lock just to extract the manager reference.
This avoids an unnecessary lock acquisition on every prompt that turns out
not to be an extension command.

Add comprehensive unit test infrastructure for RPC extension command
testing within the rpc module:
- NoopProvider: minimal stream provider that emits a single assistant
  response for testing agent turns without real model calls
- build_test_agent_session: constructs an AgentSession with NoopProvider
  and default tools for unit-level RPC testing
- build_test_rpc_options: creates RpcOptions with sensible defaults
- recv_line, parse_response, recv_response, send_recv: async RPC I/O
  helpers that handle event filtering and timeout
- assert_ok, assert_err: response assertion helpers
- recv_ui_request: waits for extension_ui_request events

Add `rpc_busy_extension_command_rejects_follow_on_extension_prompt_without_blocking`
test that verifies: when an extension command is already running (blocked
on a ui.confirm call), a second extension command prompt is immediately
rejected with an error rather than deadlocking or queuing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
